title: Otis and the Big Game
synopsis: When Otis joins his school's soccer team, he struggles with confidence and teamwork. With help from his diverse group of friends and teammates, he learns that winning isn't everything and discovers his own special talents on the field.

chapters.title: The New Team
chapters.content:
Otis Miller stared at the bright yellow flyer on the school bulletin board. 'Soccer Team Tryouts - All Welcome!' it said in big letters. His stomach felt like it had butterflies doing backflips.

'You should totally try out!' said his best friend Maya, bouncing up beside him. Her curly black hair was pulled back with a colorful headband. 'Soccer is so fun!'

Otis pushed his glasses up his nose. 'I don't know. I'm not very good at sports.'

'That's what practice is for, silly,' Maya laughed. 'Come on, I'm trying out too. We can be terrible together!'

During lunch, Otis sat with Maya and their friends at their usual table. The cafeteria was loud with kids talking and laughing.

'Did you hear about soccer tryouts?' asked Zara, taking a bite of her sandwich. Zara had moved from Somalia last year and always had the coolest braids.

'Otis is thinking about it,' Maya said, giving him an encouraging smile.

Their friend Jin looked up from his math homework. 'My older brother plays soccer. He says it's all about working together as a team.'

'See?' Maya pointed her fork at Otis. 'Teamwork! You're great at that. Remember our science project?'

Otis did remember. They had built the best volcano in third grade. But sports felt different. What if he messed up in front of everyone?

'The tryouts are after school today,' said Zara. 'Coach Rodriguez seems really nice.'

'My mom packed extra snacks,' Jin added helpfully. 'In case anyone gets hungry.'

That afternoon, Otis found himself walking toward the soccer field. His backpack felt heavy, and his new soccer cleats squeaked with each step. Maya had convinced him to at least watch.

The field was green and huge, with white lines marking the boundaries. About twenty kids were already there, stretching and kicking balls around. Coach Rodriguez, a tall woman with kind eyes and a whistle around her neck, waved them over.

'Welcome, everyone!' Coach called out. 'I'm excited to see what we can do together this season. Soccer is about having fun, learning new skills, and supporting each other.'

Otis felt his shoulders relax a little bit.

illustrations.description: Otis, a young boy with glasses and messy brown hair, stares nervously at a bright yellow soccer tryout flyer on a school bulletin board. Maya, a girl with curly black hair and a colorful headband, stands beside him with an encouraging smile. The hallway has warm golden lighting and lockers in the background.
illustrations.alt: Otis looking at soccer tryout flyer with Maya encouraging him

illustrations.description: A diverse group of children sitting at a lunch table in a bright cafeteria. Otis looks uncertain while Maya gestures enthusiastically. Zara, a girl with beautiful braids, and Jin, an Asian boy doing math homework, sit with them. Warm afternoon light streams through windows, casting golden shadows.
illustrations.alt: Friends eating lunch and discussing soccer tryouts

chapters.title: First Practice Jitters
chapters.content:
'Okay, let's start with some basic drills,' Coach Rodriguez announced. 'Find a partner and we'll practice passing.'

Maya immediately grabbed Otis's hand. 'Partners!' she declared.

They stood about ten feet apart with a black and white soccer ball between them. Maya kicked it gently toward Otis. The ball rolled slowly across the grass.

Otis tried to kick it back, but his foot barely touched it. The ball moved only two inches.

'It's okay!' Maya called out. 'Try using the inside of your foot, like this.' She demonstrated with an invisible ball.

Otis watched the other kids. A tall boy named Marcus was already really good, sending the ball exactly where he wanted. Twin sisters Emma and Ella passed back and forth like they could read each other's minds.

'Don't worry about them,' Maya said, noticing where he was looking. 'Just focus on us.'

This time, Otis used the inside of his foot like Maya showed him. The ball rolled much better, almost reaching Maya.

'Yes!' Maya cheered. 'That was awesome!'

Next, Coach had them practice dribbling around orange cones. Otis watched as the other kids zigzagged through the course. When his turn came, he moved slowly and carefully.

'Take your time, Otis,' Coach encouraged. 'Control is more important than speed.'

Behind him, he heard Marcus whisper to his friend Tyler, 'This is going to take forever.'

Otis's face turned red, but he kept going. He made it through all the cones without knocking any over.

'Great job!' said Zara, who was waiting in line behind him. 'You didn't miss a single one.'

After practice, the kids gathered around Coach Rodriguez. Everyone was sweaty and tired but smiling.

'I saw a lot of effort today,' Coach said. 'Some of you have played before, and some are brand new. That's perfect! We can all learn from each other.'

She looked around at all the faces. 'Everyone who wants to be on the team is welcome. Our first game is in three weeks against Maple Elementary.'

Otis felt his stomach flip again. A real game? Against another school?

'Don't look so scared,' Maya laughed, nudging him with her elbow. 'It'll be fun!'

Walking home, Otis wasn't so sure. What if he let the whole team down?

illustrations.description: Otis and Maya practicing passing on a green soccer field with orange cones scattered around. Otis looks concentrated as he kicks the ball with the inside of his foot. Other diverse children practice in the background. Golden afternoon sunlight creates long shadows on the grass.
illustrations.alt: Otis and Maya practicing soccer passes on the field

illustrations.description: A group of diverse children gathered around Coach Rodriguez, a tall woman with kind eyes and a whistle. Everyone looks tired but happy after practice, with soccer balls and orange cones visible on the green field. The scene has warm, encouraging lighting with earthy tones.
illustrations.alt: Team gathering around Coach Rodriguez after first practice

chapters.title: Learning the Ropes
chapters.content:
The next week, Otis started to get the hang of some basic skills. He could pass the ball to Maya most of the time now, and his dribbling was getting better.

'Today we're going to learn about positions,' Coach Rodriguez explained during Thursday's practice. 'In soccer, everyone has a special job on the field.'

She pointed to different areas marked with small flags. 'Forwards try to score goals. Midfielders help both attack and defense. Defenders protect our goal. And the goalkeeper stops the other team from scoring.'

Marcus raised his hand. 'I want to be a forward! I'm good at scoring.'

'We'll try everyone in different positions,' Coach said with a smile. 'You might be surprised where you fit best.'

They split into two small teams for a scrimmage. Otis found himself playing midfielder with Zara and Jin. Maya was on the other team as a defender.

'Stay in the middle,' Zara told him quietly. 'If the ball comes to you, just pass it to someone who's open.'

The whistle blew and the game began. Marcus immediately took off toward the goal with the ball. Tyler and Emma chased after him.

Otis hung back, watching the action move up and down the field. This was okay - he didn't have to do much.

Then suddenly, the ball bounced off someone's foot and rolled right to him. Everyone was looking at him.

'Pass it!' Jin called from his left.

'Over here!' shouted Emma from his right.

Otis panicked and kicked the ball as hard as he could. It flew over everyone's heads and landed near Maya.

'Nice clear!' called Coach Rodriguez. 'Good thinking, Otis!'

Otis blinked in surprise. He had done something right?

Maya winked at him as she dribbled the ball up the field.

For the rest of practice, Otis started to notice things. He could see when his teammates needed help. He could spot open spaces where someone could run. And when Marcus got frustrated and started hogging the ball, Otis called out helpful reminders.

'Marcus! Tyler's open!' he shouted during one play.

Marcus looked around and passed to Tyler, who scored a goal.

'Thanks, Otis!' Tyler grinned and gave him a high-five.

After practice, Jin caught up with Otis as they walked to their parents' cars.

'You're really good at seeing the whole field,' Jin said. 'My brother says that's super important.'

Otis felt a warm glow in his chest. Maybe he was finding his place on the team after all.

illustrations.description: Coach Rodriguez explaining soccer positions to a diverse group of children on a green field marked with colorful flags. She gestures toward different areas while the kids listen attentively. The scene has warm, educational lighting with golden afternoon tones and detailed grass texture.
illustrations.alt: Coach explaining soccer positions to the team

illustrations.description: Otis in the middle of a scrimmage, looking focused as he kicks the ball high over other players' heads. Maya and other diverse teammates are positioned around the field. The scene captures the action moment with dynamic movement and warm, encouraging lighting.
illustrations.alt: Otis making a clearing kick during scrimmage practice

chapters.title: The Challenge
chapters.content:
Two weeks before the big game, Coach Rodriguez made an announcement that changed everything.

'The Maple Elementary team is really good this year,' she told them during practice. 'They won their last three games by big margins. But I believe in all of you.'

Otis felt his confidence shrink. A really good team? What if they lost 10-0?

'We need to work on our teamwork,' Coach continued. 'Soccer isn't about one person being amazing. It's about eleven people working together.'

That sounded good in theory, but Marcus didn't seem to get the message. During scrimmage, he kept trying to dribble past everyone by himself.

'Pass the ball!' Ella called out when she was wide open.

But Marcus kept going and lost the ball to Maya's team.

'Dude, I was right there,' Tyler said, jogging over.

Marcus shrugged. 'I almost had it.'

Otis could see the team getting frustrated. Even Maya looked annoyed, and she was usually cheerful about everything.

During a water break, the kids gathered in small groups.

'We're never going to win if Marcus doesn't pass,' Emma whispered to her twin sister.

'Maybe Coach should talk to him,' Ella whispered back.

Zara overheard them. 'We all need to work together. That includes helping Marcus learn to trust us.'

Otis thought about this. Zara was right, but how could they help Marcus see that passing was better than going alone?

The next day at lunch, Otis had an idea.

'What if we showed Marcus how good passing can be?' he suggested to his friends.

'How?' asked Maya, curious.

'During our next scrimmage, what if we made really obvious good passes? Like, passes that lead to easy goals?'

Jin nodded slowly. 'So he can see how well it works.'

'Exactly! And maybe we can encourage him when he does pass, even if it's not perfect.'

Zara grinned. 'That's actually really smart, Otis.'

At practice that afternoon, they put their plan into action. When Otis got the ball, he immediately passed it to Jin, who passed to Emma, who scored easily.

'Great passing!' Coach called out.

When Maya got the ball, she found Zara with a perfect pass that led to another goal.

Marcus watched all this happening. When he got the ball next, he hesitated for a moment, then passed it to Tyler.

Tyler scored, and the whole team cheered.

'Nice assist, Marcus!' Otis called out.

Marcus actually smiled.

illustrations.description: Coach Rodriguez speaking seriously to the gathered team on a green field. The diverse group of children looks concerned and thoughtful as they listen to news about their upcoming challenging game. The lighting is warm but serious, with golden afternoon tones and detailed facial expressions showing worry and determination.
illustrations.alt: Coach telling the team about the challenging upcoming game

illustrations.description: Otis sitting at a lunch table with Maya, Jin, and Zara, leaning in conspiratorially as he explains his idea. The cafeteria background shows other students eating, with warm natural lighting streaming through windows. The friends look engaged and excited about the plan.
illustrations.alt: Otis explaining his teamwork plan to friends at lunch

chapters.title: Team Spirit
chapters.content:
The plan was working! Over the next few practices, Marcus started passing more often. He still tried to do too much sometimes, but he was getting better.

More importantly, the whole team was starting to feel like a real team.

'I love how you all are supporting each other,' Coach Rodriguez said during their final practice before the big game. 'That's what makes a team special.'

Otis had found his role too. He wasn't the fastest runner or the strongest kicker, but he was really good at seeing what was happening on the field and helping his teammates.

'Otis, you're like our team's eyes,' Zara said after practice. 'You always know where everyone is.'

'Yeah!' agreed Emma. 'You helped me score three goals this week by telling me when I was open.'

That evening, Otis's mom asked him about soccer while they did dishes together.

'How are you feeling about the game tomorrow?' she asked, handing him a plate to dry.

'Nervous,' Otis admitted. 'But also excited. I think our team is ready.'

'What makes you think that?' his mom asked.

Otis thought for a moment. 'We trust each other now. Like, if I pass the ball to Maya, I know she'll do something good with it. And if Marcus gets the ball, I know he'll look for ways to help the team instead of just trying to be the star.'

His mom smiled. 'That sounds like you've learned something important about teamwork.'

The next morning was Saturday - game day! Otis woke up early, his stomach full of butterflies again. But this time, they felt more like excited butterflies than scared ones.

He put on his bright blue team jersey and his lucky socks (the ones with soccer balls on them that his grandma had given him).

At breakfast, his dad made his favorite pancakes shaped like soccer balls.

'For good luck,' his dad said with a wink.

'Thanks, Dad. But I think we make our own luck by working together.'

When Otis arrived at the school field, all his teammates were already there. Everyone looked nervous and excited at the same time.

Maya bounced over to him. 'Ready for this?'

'Ready,' Otis said, and he actually meant it.

Coach Rodriguez gathered everyone in a circle.

'Remember,' she said, 'win or lose, I'm proud of how much you've all grown as players and as teammates. Now let's go have some fun!'

illustrations.description: The team in a circle around Coach Rodriguez during their final practice before the big game. All the diverse children look focused and determined, wearing their practice clothes. The golden afternoon light creates a warm, unifying atmosphere on the green field with soccer balls scattered nearby.
illustrations.alt: Final team meeting before the big game

illustrations.description: Otis at the breakfast table with his dad, who is serving soccer ball-shaped pancakes. Otis wears his bright blue team jersey and looks excited rather than nervous. Morning sunlight streams through the kitchen window, creating a warm, supportive family atmosphere with golden tones.
illustrations.alt: Otis eating soccer ball pancakes on game day morning

chapters.title: The Big Game
chapters.content:
The Maple Elementary team looked intimidating when they got off their bus. They were wearing matching green uniforms and looked very serious.

'They're huge!' whispered Tyler.

'Size isn't everything,' said Maya firmly. 'We've got something they might not have - teamwork.'

Otis nodded. Maya was right. Their team had something special now.

Coach Rodriguez announced the starting lineup. Otis would be playing midfielder, just like he'd been practicing.

'Remember,' she told them as they walked onto the field, 'play your positions, trust your teammates, and have fun.'

The referee blew the whistle, and the game began!

Maple Elementary took control of the ball right away. Their players were fast and skilled. Within five minutes, they had scored their first goal.

'It's okay!' called out Jin from his defender position. 'We've got this!'

Otis's team didn't give up. When they got the ball back, they started doing what they did best - working together.

Otis got a pass from Zara and immediately saw Marcus making a run down the right side. Instead of trying to dribble himself, Otis passed the ball perfectly to Marcus.

Marcus took the pass, looked up, and saw Tyler open near the goal. Marcus passed it over, and Tyler scored!

'YES!' shouted the whole team, running to celebrate together.

The game stayed close. Maple Elementary scored another goal, but then Emma scored for Otis's team after a beautiful passing play that involved five different players.

By halftime, the score was 2-2.

'You're playing amazingly!' Coach Rodriguez told them during the break. 'Keep doing what you're doing. Trust each other.'

The second half was even more exciting. Both teams had chances to score. Otis made several great passes that helped his team attack, and he also dropped back to help Jin and Zara defend when they needed it.

With five minutes left in the game, Maple Elementary scored to make it 3-2.

Otis could see some of his teammates starting to get discouraged, but he remembered what he'd learned about being the team's eyes.

'We can do this!' he called out. 'Maya, get ready to run up the left side! Marcus, stay wide on the right!'

When his team got the ball back, they executed a perfect play. Otis got the ball in midfield and saw exactly what he'd been telling them to do. Maya was running up the left, completely open.

He sent a long pass to Maya, who crossed it to Marcus, who passed it to Emma, who scored with thirty seconds left!

3-3! The game ended in a tie, but it felt like a victory.

illustrations.description: The opening kickoff of the soccer game with both teams lined up on opposite sides. Otis's team in bright blue faces Maple Elementary in green uniforms. Parents and spectators fill the sidelines under golden afternoon light, creating an exciting game day atmosphere on the detailed grass field.
illustrations.alt: The big game beginning with both teams on the field

illustrations.description: The climactic moment as Emma scores the tying goal with thirty seconds left. Players from both teams are in dynamic action poses around the goal. Otis is visible in the background, having made the key pass. The scene captures the excitement with warm, dramatic lighting and detailed expressions of effort and determination.
illustrations.alt: Emma scoring the dramatic tying goal

chapters.title: Champions
chapters.content:
Both teams lined up to shake hands after the game. The Maple Elementary players were good sports.

'Great game,' said their captain, a tall girl with a ponytail. 'You guys really work well together.'

'Thanks,' said Marcus, grinning. 'We've been practicing teamwork a lot.'

After the other team left, Otis's team gathered around Coach Rodriguez.

'I am so proud of all of you,' she said, her eyes bright with happiness. 'You played with heart, you supported each other, and you never gave up.'

'Even though we didn't win?' asked Tyler.

'You did win,' Coach said. 'Maybe not the scoreboard, but you won something more important. You learned how to be a real team.'

Otis looked around at his teammates - Maya bouncing excitedly, Zara braiding Emma's hair, Jin sharing snacks with everyone, Marcus giving high-fives, Tyler doing a silly victory dance.

They had become more than just a soccer team. They had become friends.

'Plus,' added Ella with a big smile, 'we didn't lose either!'

Everyone laughed at that.

Coach Rodriguez had one more surprise. 'I got something for each of you,' she said, pulling out a bag. She handed each player a small medal that said 'Team Spirit Champions.'

'You might not have the highest score,' she explained, 'but you have the biggest hearts.'

Otis held his medal and felt proud in a way he'd never felt before. Not because he was the best player - he wasn't. But because he had found his place on the team and helped make everyone better.

'Next week we play Roosevelt Elementary,' Coach announced. 'But for now, let's celebrate!'

As the team packed up their equipment, Maya came over to Otis.

'I'm so glad you joined the team,' she said. 'You made all of us better players.'

'You too,' Otis replied. 'I never would have tried out without you encouraging me.'

Walking to his parents' car, Otis thought about everything that had happened. A few weeks ago, he had been scared to even try soccer. Now he couldn't wait for the next game.

'How was it, superstar?' his mom asked as he got in the car.

'It was perfect,' Otis said, holding up his medal. 'We tied the game, but we won something even better.'

'What's that?' his dad asked.

'We learned how to be a real team. And that's the best win of all.'

As they drove home, Otis was already thinking about next week's practice. He had some ideas for new plays that could help the team work together even better.

After all, that's what team captains do - and Otis was beginning to think he might be captain material after all.

illustrations.description: Both teams shaking hands in a line after the game, showing good sportsmanship. The diverse players look tired but happy, with parents and spectators in the background. Golden late afternoon light creates a warm, celebratory atmosphere on the grass field with scattered soccer equipment.
illustrations.alt: Teams shaking hands after the game showing good sportsmanship

illustrations.description: Coach Rodriguez presenting 'Team Spirit Champions' medals to the gathered team. All the diverse children look excited and proud, holding their medals up in the golden evening light. The scene captures joy and achievement with warm, celebrating tones and detailed happy expressions.
illustrations.alt: Coach giving out Team Spirit Champions medals to the team

metadata.illustrationStyle: Warm, inviting adventure illustration style. Earthy tones, golden light. Detailed environments with hidden details kids can discover.

questions.question: Why was Otis nervous about joining the soccer team?
choices: He thought he wasn't good at sports
He was afraid of Coach Rodriguez
questions.explanation: Otis told Maya 'I'm not very good at sports' when she encouraged him to try out for the soccer team.

questions.question: What special skill did Otis discover he had?
choices: Seeing the whole field and helping teammates
questions.explanation: Jin told Otis 'You're really good at seeing the whole field,' and Zara called him 'our team's eyes' because he could see what was happening and help his teammates.

questions.question: What was Marcus's problem at first?
choices: He wouldn't pass the ball to teammates
He didn't want to play defense
He was afraid of the other team
questions.explanation: Marcus kept trying to dribble past everyone by himself and wouldn't pass the ball, even when teammates were open.

questions.question: How did Otis help Marcus learn to be a better teammate?
choices: He told the coach to bench Marcus
He showed how good passing works and encouraged Marcus when he did pass
questions.explanation: Otis came up with a plan to show Marcus how effective passing could be, and the team encouraged Marcus when he made good passes.

questions.question: What was the final score of the big game?
questions.explanation: The game ended 3-3 when Emma scored with thirty seconds left to tie the game.

questions.question: What medals did Coach Rodriguez give the team?
questions.explanation: Coach Rodriguez gave each player a medal that said 'Team Spirit Champions' because they had learned to work together as a real team.

questions.question: What did Otis learn was most important about soccer?
questions.explanation: Throughout the story, Otis learned that teamwork was the most important part of soccer, and at the end he said learning to be a real team was 'the best win of all.'

pages.title: Complete the Team Talk
pages.instructions: Fill in the blanks with the correct words from Otis and the Big Game!
pages.content:
Coach Rodriguez told the team, 'Soccer isn't about one person being ___1___. It's about eleven people working ___2___ as a ___3___.'

Otis discovered he was really good at ___4___ the whole field and helping his ___5___ know when they were ___6___.

In the big game, Otis's team learned that even though they didn't win, they had won something more ___7___ - they learned how to be a real ___8___.

pages.title: Soccer Field Positions
pages.instructions: Help Otis remember the soccer positions by filling in the blanks!
pages.content:
Coach Rodriguez explained the different positions on the soccer field:

___1___ try to score goals and play near the other team's goal.

___2___ help both attack and defense and play in the middle of the field.

___3___ protect our goal and try to stop the other team from scoring.

The ___4___ is the only player who can use their hands to stop the ball from going into the goal.
